observations.recommendation: c. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to take proper action on the government properties that are for disposal to prevent their continued deterioration and decline in value leading to loss of possible income from disposal.
observations.reason: Partial disposal of unserviceable properties was done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to ensure all disbursements be supported with complete documentation in accordance with Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: On-going processing of Lot No. 23.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to review the Memoranda of Agreement (MOA) pertaining to the above fund transfers and make representations with source agencies on the necessary actions to be taken for funds received but for which intended programs, projects, and activities remain unimplemented, or if implemented, were not completed or abandoned, otherwise, revert the unutilized balances to the source agency in accordance with existing rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Unliquidated balance remained for verification.

observations.recommendation: a. Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from the current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance;
observations.reason: Balances from CY 2023 and earlier balances remained unutilized.

observations.recommendation: b. Ensure that the programs, projects, and activities related to disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness are implemented within the scheduled timeframes.
observations.reason: Programs, Projects and activities remained unimplemented or were implemented beyond the budgeted year.

observations.recommendation: 1. The Municipal Assessor, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and all concerned to work for the immediate titling, recording, or transfer of ownership of all land belonging to the Municipality to establish ownership and avoid risk in court litigation from the previous owners who are interested in taking back the properties.
observations.reason: On-going titling of remaining lots.

observations.recommendation: 2. Record all acquired real properties in the books of the Municipality upon securing proper and valid papers to support the documentation or titling to present an accurate land account in the financial statement.
observations.reason: On-going titling of remaining lots.

observations.recommendation: 3. Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
observations.reason: Process of one-time cleansing is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and property cards respectively, compute depreciation and require them to reconcile these records regularly to have a complete verifiable, accurate and reliable PPE balances as presented in the Statement of Financial Position. Moreover, the Inventory Committee conduct annually a complete physical count of its PPEs.
observations.reason: On-going preparation and reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Disposal Committee take proper action on government properties that are for disposal to prevent their continued deterioration and decline in value leading to loss of possible income from disposal.
observations.reason: Partial disposal of unserviceable properties was done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned offices to perform their assigned duties and to coordinate with each other in order to facilitate the proper accounting and reporting of Local Road Network account on the financial statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.reason: The agency is currently studying the guidelines to implement them. Moreover, coordination and documentation is on-going.

observations.recommendation: a. The municipality shall request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to the recipient beneficiaries and Certificate of Acceptance to be issued, and other documents required from the Office of the Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance of the audit observation.
b. The Office of the Municipal Engineer should provide necessary documents for the turn-over and acceptance of completed projects already used by the recipient schools and LGUs.
observations.reason: For schools, records are incomplete to identify the specific beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: c. The Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the dropping of affected PPE account in the agency’s book and facilitate the transfer of records to the beneficiaries by taking up the assets received by them.
observations.reason: The JEV will be prepared once transfers of PPEs are made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU – Patnongon, Antique fast-track the remaining core shelter units and upon completion, immediately award to family beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Seven Core Shelter Units remains to be constructed and awarded to beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU submit the required updated lacking supporting documents per Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 dated February 28, 2005.
observations.reason: On-going process of titling.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer, and the Inventory Committee to
coordinate in reconciling the PPEs records in the Financial Statements and the
Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment, and
to work together for the dropping of PPEs that are no longer in the
possession and ownership of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to conduct collation of complete documentation and
verification of the Property, Plant and Equipment, thereafter, make
necessary journal entries to transfer the completed and in-use Property,
Plant, and Equipment from the Trust Fund Books to the General Fund
Books in accordance with current existing regulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Treasurer and MEEDO Head to strictly implement the provisions in the
Lease Contract Agreement for Market Stall regarding the timely collection
of stall rental payments, the imposition of surcharges for delayed payments,
and the closure of stalls for stallholders with two months of delayed
payments.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to henceforth record transactions pertaining to the grant of
cash advances to accountable officers and employees for special
purpose/time-bound undertakings properly, pursuant to Section 112 of PD
No. 1445 and Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01,
2015.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
and the Sangguniang Bayan coordinate for the formulation and
establishment of internal guidelines on the granting of the AICS Program
for the proper and efficient utilization of the Municipality’s resources.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from the current year’s
appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance; and
b. Ensure that the programs, projects, and activities related to disaster
prevention, mitigation, and preparedness are implemented within the
scheduled timeframes.

a. Reassess the composition of the Inventory and Disposal Committee in consideration of the regular duties and responsibilities, as well as their additional duties and responsibilities as Committee members;

b. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to ensure that the complete physical count of PPEs be done within three months or less in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present the reliable data pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchase of the Accountable Forms as assets using the Inventory accounting following the Perpetual Inventory System, and to record to “Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers” account following the Perpetual Inventory System, and to record to “Accountable Forms Expense” account the cost of Accountable Forms issued to end-users.
a. The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular review and reconciliation reports and supporting documents for recording of RPT, SET, and their corresponding discounts;
b. The Municipal Accountant to timely record discounts based on the report submitted by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and Management agreed to formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations and submit the fully accomplished AAPSI and copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 99 of RA No. 11936.

We commended the Management, especially the MENRO, in their implementation and enforcement of efficient and effective solid waste management and the enforcement of policies aimed in maintaining sanitation of the Municipality. Further, we recommended and Management agreed to continue and remain compliant with the above stated provisions and other rules and regulations regarding the delivery of effective and efficient system of solid waste management, and to formulate additional innovative programs that would minimize the volume of solid waste.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue and remain compliant with the above stated provisions and other rules and regulations regarding the utilization of 20% Development Fund. Furthermore, ensure the programs projects and activities that constitute the 20% Development Fund must be well planned and implementation ready.

1. Create an Inventory Committee whose members shall be temporarily relieved of all regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts until the same is completed;
observations.reason: (Implemented in CY 2023)

observations.recommendation: 2. Prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and complete the physical inventory in three months or less, in order to come up with reliable PPE account balances, pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006;

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to ensure the timely submission of copies of perfected government contracts and purchase orders and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from the date of execution, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive comply to Section 5.2 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC JMC No. 2014-1 on the Implementing Guidelines for the Establishment of LDRRMOs in the Local Government Units (LGUs). We also recommended that the Management explore the options on how to augment the staff of the LDRRMO, if necessary.

We recommended to the Management the following:
 
1.	The Municipal Assessor, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and all concerned to work for the immediate titling, recording, or transfer of ownership of all land belonging to the Municipality to establish ownership and avoid risk in court litigation from the previous owners who are interested in taking back the properties.

2.	Record all acquired real properties in the books of the Municipality upon securing proper and valid papers to support the documentation or titling to present an accurate land account in the financial statement
observations.reason: Some prior years’ purchases of land were unreconciled due to missing records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

1.	Create an Inventory Committee whose members shall be temporarily relieved of all regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking for the one-time cleansing of PPE until the same is completed;

2.	Prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and complete the physical inventory in three months or less, in order to come up with reliable PPE account balances, pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and 

3.	Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and purchase orders and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Engineer to forward the contracts entered by the LGU to the BAC for submission to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and property cards, respectively, compute depreciation and require them to reconcile these records regularly to have a verifiable, accurate and reliable PPE balances as presented in the Statement of Financial Position. Moreover, the Inventory Committee conduct annually a complete physical count of its PPEs.
observations.managementAction: On-going preparation of PPELC and property cards. Will be completed along with the conduct of the one-time cleansing.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Disposal Committee take proper action on government properties that are for disposal to prevent their continued deterioration and decline in value leading to loss of possible income from disposal.

On-going preparation of documents of properties for disposal.

For those with complete documentation, partial disposal may be done.

We also recommended that the Management explore the options on how to augment the staff of the LDRRMO, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality added one staff at the office of the LDRRMO. As of AAR date, only one staff is lacking in the said office.

The Office of the Municipal Engineer should provide necessary documents for the turn-over and acceptance of completed projects already used by the recipient schools and LGUs.


The Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the dropping of affected PPE account in the agency’s book and facilitate the transfer of records to the beneficiaries by taking up the assets received by them.

observations.recommendation: 2.10. 	We recommended and Management agreed to ensure all disbursements be supported with complete documentation in accordance with Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated July 12, 2012.

3.10. 	We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present the reliable data pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

4.10. 	We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchase of the Accountable Forms as assets using the “Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers” account following the Perpetual Inventory System, and to record to “Accountable Forms Expense” account the cost of Accountable Forms issued to end-users.

5.13. 	We recommended and Management agreed to direct:
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular review and reconciliation reports and supporting documents for recording of RPT, SET, and their corresponding discounts; and
The Municipal Account to timely record discounts based on the report submitted by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

6.13. 	We recommended and Management agreed to review the Memoranda of Agreement (MOA) pertaining to the above fund transfers and make representations with source agencies on the necessary actions to be taken for funds received but for which intended programs, projects, and activities remain unimplemented, or if implemented, were not completed or abandoned, otherwise, revert the unutilized balances to the source agency in accordance with existing rules and regulations.

7.9. 	We recommended and Management agreed to formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations and submit the fully accomplished AAPSI to COA within the reglementary period copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 99 of RA No. 11936.

8.9. 	We commended the Management, especially the MENRO, in their implementation and enforcement of efficient and effective system of solid waste management and the enforcement of policies aimed in maintaining the sanitation of the Municipality. Further, we recommended and Management agreed to continue and remain compliant with the above stated provisions and other rules and regulations regarding the delivery of effective and efficient system of solid waste management, and to formulate additional innovative programs that would minimize the volume of solid waste.

9.10. 	We commended the Management for its efficient delivery of socio-economic developmental projects for the benefits of its constituents. We recommended and Management agreed to continue and remain compliant with the above stated provisions and other rules and regulations regarding the utilization of 20% Development Fund. Furthermore, ensure the programs projects and activities that constitute the 20% Development Fund must we well planned and implementation ready.

10.12. 	We recommended and Management agreed to:
Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from the current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance; and
Ensure that the programs, projects, and activities related to disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness are implemented within the scheduled timeframes.

We commended the Management for their initiative and commitment in attaining 195 percent collection efficiency on real property taxes. Moreover, we recommend that the Management continue their initiative and commitment towards higher collection efficiency.

We recommended the following:

a.	The Municipal Mayor and the heads of the receiving municipal department/office inform the Municipal Accountant whenever they receive donations in kind from other government agencies and private individuals so that the property, plant and equipment (PPE) or semi-expendable properties received and their related revenues be properly recognized, measured, and recorded in the Municipality's books of accounts; and

b.	The Municipal Accountant, based on the information and documents provided by the Municipal Mayor and/or the heads of the receiving municipal department/office and the fair values of the received PPE or semi-expendable properties, record the received assets and its related revenues in the Municipality's books of accounts by debiting the Specific PPE or semi-expendable property accounts and crediting the Grants and Donations in Kind account with account number 4-04-02-020. Specifically, for the unrecorded receipt of donations in kind in CY 2022.

We recommended that:

1.	The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant an annual Schedule of the Delinquent Stallholders as at year-end; and  

2.	The Municipal Accountant record in the books of accounts the receivables on the market stalls rental fees based on the report forwarded by the Municipal Treasurer and the corresponding income earned therefrom

We recommended that the Management:

1.	Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from the current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance; and

2.	Ensure that the programs, projects, and activities related to disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness are implemented within the scheduled timeframes

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure the execution or renewal of the lease contracts for market stalls before tenants are allowed to occupy or continue to occupy the market stalls. After which, copies of the contracts be submitted to the Auditor within five working days from the execution for auditorial and legal review.

We recommended that:

1.	The Municipal Engineer and all other implementing offices of the Municipality expedite the procurement and implementation process so that the services and benefits from these projects are delivered to the constituents without unnecessary delay; and

2.	The Local Development Council conduct a careful and well-planned identification of development projects and choose implementation ready PPAs to be funded from the 20% DF so the desired level of efficiency is achieved

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and purchase orders and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer strictly enforce the submission of RCI together with its disbursement vouchers and RCD with its supporting documents to COA within fifteen (15) days after the end of the month.

We also recommended that the Management identify root causes of delayed preparation and submission of DVs and RCDs and place necessary controls addressing these root causes to ensure timely submission.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and property cards, respectively, compute depreciation and require them to reconcile these records regularly to have a verifiable, accurate and reliable PPE balances as presented in the Statement of Financial Position. Moreover, the Inventory Committee conduct annually a complete physical count of its PPEs.

In view of the above, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Disposal Committee take proper action on government properties that are for disposal to prevent their continued deterioration and decline in value leading to loss of possible income from disposal.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Engineer together with Resident Engineer to closely monitor the proper implementation of the Occupational Safety and Health Standards and Programs for construction projects to ensure the safety of every worker employed therein, as well as the general public.
Ensure the inclusion of Occupational Safety and Health provisions in the preparation of the Program of Works and consequently be included in the contract.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive strictly enforce the regular preparation of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF by the Municipal Accountant and the submission of the said report to the Auditor by the LDRRMO through the LDC and the LDRRMC on or before the 15thday after the end of each month as required in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive comply to Section 5.2 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC JMC No. 2014-1 on the Implementing Guidelines for the Establishment of LDRRMOs in the Local Government Units (LGUs).
We also recommended that the Management explore the options on how to augment the staff of the LDRRMO, if necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and all other implementing offices of the Municipality expedite the implementation of all projects funded from the 20% DF to ensure optimum utilization of the fund so that the services and benefits from these projects are delivered to the constituents without unnecessary delay.
We also recommended and Management agreed that the Local Development Council conduct a careful and well planned identification of development projects and choose an implementation ready PPAs to be funded from the 20% DF so the desired level of efficiency is achieved.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct an annual physical count of the LGU’s PPEs pursuant to Section 102 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Section 266, Volume I of Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation in accordance with Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended the following,

The Municipal Treasurer cancel the unclaimed stale checks and return the original disbursement voucher to the Municipal Accountant in order to effect the necessary adjusting entries. For stale checks in the hand of the payee, send written notices reminding them of the need to present stale checks for cancellation before replacement can be made.

The Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjusting entries to record the stale checks.

We recommended that the Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the PMR of the Municipality and submit it to the GPPB within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance to Section 12.2 of Revised IRR of RA 9184 and GPPB Circular 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.

We recommended the following: 

Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance.


Make sure that the programs, projects and activities related to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness be implemented within the scheduled timeframes.

We recommended that the Management require the GAD Focal Point to furnish the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report as provided for in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 in order to verify on whether gender issues contained in the GAD Plan and Budget were fully addressed.

We reiterated to recommend that the Local Chief Executive require: 
The Inventory Committee to convene and appropriately plan the steps to conduct a complete physical count of all the property, plant and equipment of the Municipality and prepare the required RPCPPE; and
(Fully Implemented in 2020)

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records for PPE accounts by determining the cause of variance and effect the proper adjustments thereof in order to provide a reliable data on the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned offices to perform their assigned duties and to coordinate with each other in order to facilitate the proper accounting and reporting of Local Road Network account on the financial statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the Municipality through its Local Chief Executive optimally utilize the 20 percent DF by:

Instructing concerned officials to carefully evaluate the programs and projects to be appropriated in the 20 percent DF with due consideration the same can be implemented as scheduled; and
(Fully Implemented in 2020)

Directing the involved offices to fast track the implementation of programs and projects under the 20 percent DF of prior years.

We recommended that the:

Municipality through the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all the properties and prepare the required RPCPPE pursuant to Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and


Inventory report be reconciled with the PPE accounts in the financial statements in order to establish the reliability of the balances presented.

We recommended that:

The municipality shall request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to the respective beneficiaries.  


The Office of the Municipal Engineer should provide necessary documents for the turn-over and acceptance of completed projects already used by the recipient schools and LGUs.

The Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the dropping of affected PPE account in the agency’s book and facilitate the transfer of records to the beneficiaries by taking up the assets received by them.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

If funds warrant, hire qualified personnel to occupy three (3) mandatory staff position of MDRRM Office following the composition and structure set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121.
We recommended that the LGU – Patnongon, Antique fast-track the construction of the remaining core shelter units and upon completion, immediately award to family beneficiaries.

We recommended that the LGU submit the required updated lacking supporting documents per Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 dated February 28, 2005.
We recommended that the Local Government Unit exert effort to have the purchased lots titled in the name of the municipality to establish ownership of the land.

We recommended that:

The municipality shall request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to the respective beneficiaries.  

The Office of the Municipal Engineer should provide necessary documents for the turn-over and acceptance of completed projects already used by the recipient schools and LGUs.

The Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the dropping of affected PPE account in the agency’s book and facilitate the transfer of records to the beneficiaries by taking up the assets received by them.

On-going collation and checking of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct an annual physical count of the LGU’s PPEs pursuant to Section 102 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Section 266, Volume I of Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation in accordance with Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended the following,
The Municipal Treasurer cancel the unclaimed stale checks and return the original disbursement voucher to the Municipal Accountant in order to effect the necessary adjusting entries. For stale checks in the hand of the payee, send written notices reminding them of the need to present stale checks for cancellation before replacement can be made.
The Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjusting entries to record the stale checks.

In view of the above observations, we recommended that the Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the PMR of the Municipality and submit it to the GPPB within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance to Section 12.2 of Revised IRR of RA 9184 and GPPB Circular 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.

In view of the above, under normal circumstances, we recommended the following:
Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance.
Make sure that the programs, projects and activities related to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness be implemented within the scheduled timeframes.

We recommended that the Management require the GAD Focal Point to furnish the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report as provided for in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 in order to verify on whether gender issues contained in the GAD Plan and Budget were fully addressed.

We reiterated to recommend that the Local Chief Executive require: 
The Inventory Committee to convene and appropriately plan the steps to conduct a complete physical count of all the property, plant and equipment of the Municipality and prepare the required RPCPPE; and

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records for PPE accounts by determining the cause of variance and effect the proper adjustments thereof in order to provide a reliable data on the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The municipality shall request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to the respective beneficiaries.  

The Office of the Municipal Engineer should provide necessary documents for the turn-over and acceptance of completed projects already used by the recipient schools and LGUs.

The Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the dropping of affected PPE account in the agency’s book and facilitate the transfer of records to the beneficiaries by taking up the assets received by them.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already identified some of the PPEs but failed to transfer the assets to recipient schools or LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU – Patnongon, Antique fast-track the construction of the remaining core shelter units and upon completion, immediately award to family beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The project has completed six (6) units, 12 units are on-going, and the remaining seven (7) units are still to be implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated to recommend that the Local Chief Executive require:
The Inventory Committee to convene and appropriately plan the steps to conduct a complete physical count of all the property, plant and equipment of the Municipality and prepare the required RPCPPE; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records for PPE accounts by determining the cause of variance and effect the proper adjustments thereof in order to provide a reliable data on the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned offices to perform their assigned duties and to coordinate with each other in order to facilitate the proper accounting and reporting of Local Road Network account on the financial statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the Municipality through its Local Chief Executive optimally utilize the 20 percent DF by:
Instructing concerned officials to carefully evaluate the programs and projects to be appropriated in the 20 percent DF with due consideration the same can be implemented as scheduled; and
Directing the involved offices to fast track the implementation of programs and projects under the 20 percent DF of prior years.

We recommended that the Municipality establish its GAD Database as required by RA No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women and in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 in order to come up a realistic and complete GAD Plan that addresses the gender issues and concerns of the locality.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly follow the NGAS provisions on recording and updating of cashbooks as a tool in monitoring the flow of cash transactions and to avoid the possibility of incurring unnecessary financial expenses and accumulation of collections.  Likewise, cashbooks should be regularly reconciled with the accounting records for timely detection and correction of errors.

We recommended that the Management efficiently implement the planned programs, projects or activities contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan during the year to attain the objectives pursuant to DILG MC No. 2012-73.  Likewise, require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate the LDRRMFIP, if needed, identify the items and concerns that constraints the implementation and determine measures to attain the desired DRRM objectives.

We recommended that the Management:

Accomplish a perfected Contract of Lease between the Municipality and the tenants of market stalls specifying the rates, durations and other conditions based on the Municipal Tax Ordinance No. 3 Series of 2016; and 

Provide the needed equipment to ensure proper collection of fees relating to slaughter house operations.

We recommended that the Management:

Require the supplier to refund in full the amount equivalent to the computed liquidated damages;

Deduct from the supplier’s succeeding payments, if there is any, the amount equivalent to their previous liquidated damages; and

Impose liquidated damages to suppliers who failed to deliver the goods based on the schedule indicated in the PO without the approved written order for time extension or to suspend the delivery schedule from the procuring entity pursuant to the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the MDRRM Officer to have coordination with other concerned offices in the Municipality in order to fully implement the identified PAPs and to maximize the utilization of DRRM Fund within the scheduled period.

If funds warrant, hire qualified personnel to occupy three (3) mandatory staff position of MDRRM Office following the composition and structure set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121; and

Designate or reassign permanent personnel of the LGU to MDRRM Office in concurrent capacity to handle and accomplish various programs, activities and projects pursuant to Item 5.5 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that management exert effort in securing the records of the Other Receivables which remained outstanding for more than fifteen (15) years and enforce its collection.  In the event that its collections is improbable, procedures prescribed pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 should be followed.

We recommend that the LGU submit the required updated lacking supporting documents per Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 dated February 28, 2005.
observations.managementAction: There is ongoing follow up of applications for special patent.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive exert effort to follow up the submission of the supporting documents and the liquidation report of the NGOs/POs in compliance with Section 5.4 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.
We recommend that the Local Government Unit exert effort to have the purchased lots titled in the name of the municipality to establish ownership of the land.
observations.managementAction: There is ongoing follow up to locate some documents needed for transfer of title and the applications for Special Patent is on process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
Municipality, through the Inventory Committee, conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all the properties and prepare the required RPCPPE pursuant to Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
Inventory report be reconciled with the PPE accounts in the financial statements in order to establish the reliability of the balances presented.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly follow the NGAS provisions on recording and updating of cashbooks as a tool in monitoring the flow of cash transactions and to avoid the possibility of incurring unnecessary financial expenses and accumulation of collections.  Likewise, cashbooks should be regularly reconciled with the accounting records for timely detection and correction of errors.

We recommended that the Management efficiently implement the planned programs, projects or activities contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan during the year to attain the objectives pursuant to DILG MC No. 2012-73.  Likewise, require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate the LDRRMFIP, if needed, identify the items and concerns that constraints the implementation and determine measures to attain the desired DRRM objectives.

We recommended that the Management:
Accomplish a perfected Contract of Lease between the Municipality and the tenants of market stalls specifying the rates, durations and other conditions based on the Municipal Tax Ordinance No. 3 Series of 2016; and
Provide the needed equipment to ensure proper collection of fees relating to slaughter house operations.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the supplier to refund in full the amount equivalent to the computed liquidated damages;
Deduct from the supplier’s succeeding payments, if there is any, the amount equivalent to their previous liquidated damages; and
Impose liquidated damages to suppliers who failed to deliver the goods based on the schedule indicated in the PO without the approved written order for time extension or to suspend the delivery schedule from the procuring entity pursuant to the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that:

The municipality shall request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to the respective beneficiaries.  

The Office of the Municipal Engineer should provide necessary documents for the turn-over and acceptance of completed projects already used by the recipient schools and LGUs.

The Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the dropping of affected PPE account in the agency’s book and facilitate the transfer of records to the beneficiaries by taking up the assets received by them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct all concerned officials involved in the planning and implementation of 20 percent DF to evaluate the necessity, feasibility and timing of programs and projects implementation in the ensuing year to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the MDRRM Officer to have coordination with other concerned offices in the Municipality in order to fully implement the identified PAPs and to maximize the utilization of DRRM Fund within the scheduled period.

If funds warrant, hire qualified personnel to occupy three (3) mandatory staff position of MDRRM Office following the composition and structure set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121; and

Designate or reassign permanent personnel of the LGU to MDRRM Office in concurrent capacity to handle and accomplish various programs, activities and projects pursuant to Item 5.5 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction:
The PPAs for Disaster Risk Reduction and Management were not fully implemented on the year appropriated and accomplishment was reduced in the current year.

The observation was first raised in CY 2017 AAR and reiterated in Part II – 3 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality:

Should close the open dumpsite pursuant to Republic Act No. 9003;  

Appropriate funds to support the ESWM Program by procuring a site and necessary equipment needed for the operation of Sanitary Landfill, as a replacement of the open dumpsite; and

Request additional fund from the National Solid Waste Management Commission, based on the approved ESWM Plan, for products, facilities, technologies and processes to enhance proper solid waste management.

We recommended that the agency transfer the SEF intended for sports development directly to the DepEd as a supplementary budget need for the operation of public schools, in order to subject the utilization for timely audit.  Likewise, charges against the SEF should be supported with documents and properly evaluated as to legality of the claim.

We recommended that management exert effort in securing the records of the Other Receivables which remained outstanding for more than fifteen (15) years and enforce its collection.  In the event that its collections are improbable, procedures prescribed pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 should be followed.
observations.managementAction: Some of the required documents to support the Request for Write-off were not available.

Some of the required documents to support the Request for Write-off were not available.

observations.managementAction: There is ongoing follow up to locate some documents needed for transfer of title and the applications for Special Patent is on process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mobilize the concerned offices of the LGU to fast track the implementation of various development projects contained in the Annual Investment Plan in accordance with the planned schedule of implementation and in conformity with the approved plans and specifications.  Likewise, the management should maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, program and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.
observations.reason: The audit observation was first raised in CY 2012 AAR and reiterated in Part II - 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The municipality shall request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to the respective beneficiaries.
The Office of the Municipal Engineer should provide necessary documents for the turn-over and acceptance of completed projects already used by the recipient schools and LGUs.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the dropping of affected PPE account in the agency’s book and facilitate the transfer of records to the beneficiaries by taking up the assets received by them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all concerned officials involved in the planning and implementation of 20 percent DF to evaluate the necessity, feasibility and timing of programs and projects implementation in the ensuing year to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the MDRRM Officer to have coordination with other concerned offices in the Municipality in order to fully implement the identified PAPs and to maximize the utilization of DRRM Fund within the scheduled period.
If funds warrant, hire qualified personnel to occupy three (3) mandatory staff position of MDRRM Office following the composition and structure set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121; and
Designate or reassign permanent personnel of the LGU to MDRRM Office in concurrent capacity to handle and accomplish various programs, activities and projects pursuant to Item 5.5 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipality:
Should close the open dumpsite pursuant to Republic Act No. 9003;
Appropriate funds to support the ESWM Program by procuring a site and necessary equipment needed for the operation of a Sanitary Landfill, as a replacement of the open dumpsite; and
Request additional fund from the National Solid Waste Management Commission, based on the approved ESWM Plan, for products, facilities, technologies and processes to enhance proper solid waste management.

We recommended that the agency transfer the SEF intended for sports development directly to the DepEd as a supplementary budget need for the operation of public schools, in order to subject the utilization for timely audit.  Likewise, charges against the SEF should be supported with documents and properly evaluated as to legality of the claim.

We recommended that the municipality create a team to conduct the inventory of property, plant and equipment, and prepare the required RPCPPE to be the basis for reconciliation with the accounting records.  Likewise, the conduct of physical inventory should be given attention in the incoming years in order to determine the actual status of the assets, whether still serviceable or should be disposed following the prescribed procedures.

By reason of non-implementation of the same prior year audit recommendation, we reiterated that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly observe the general limitations on Personal Services cited under Section 325 (a) of RA No. 7160.  Likewise, due care and diligence should be exercised in the formulation of Personal Services component of the annual budget.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive being the MDRRMC Chairman direct the MDRRMO-Designate and other concerned officials to maximize the utilization of 70 percent Mitigation Fund by giving preference to the implementation of disaster related programs, projects and activities as planned in order to address any adverse effect of disaster.
observations.reason: The audit observation was first raised in CY 2016 AAR and reiterated in Part II - 3 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC Chairperson to adopt competitive bidding as the general method of procurement in the procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants, and alternative methods shall be resorted only in highly exceptional cases with strict adherence to the prescribed requisites.

We recommended that management exert effort in securing the records of the Other Receivables which remained outstanding for more than fifteen (15) years and enforce its collection.  In the event that its collections is improbable, procedures prescribed pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 should be followed.
observations.managementAction:
Resolution No. 196-2016 dated October 18, 2016 was passed approving the write-off of long outstanding Other Receivables.  

There is on-going preparation of documents to support the Request for Write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU – Patnongon, Antique fast-track the construction of the remaining core shelter units and upon completion, immediately award to family beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The project has completed six (6) units, 12 units are on-going, and the remaining seven (7) units are still to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU submit the required updated lacking supporting documents per Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 dated February 28, 2005.
observations.managementAction: There is ongoing follow up of applications for special patent.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive exert effort to follow up the submission of the supporting documents and the liquidation report of the NGOs/POs in compliance with Section 5.4 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.
observations.managementAction: The request for write-off KASIPA account is on-process.
observations.reason: The finding was first raised in CY 2012 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Government Unit exert effort to have the purchased lots titled in the name of the municipality to establish ownership of the land.
observations.managementAction: There is ongoing follow up to locate some documents needed for transfer of title and the applications for Special Patent is on process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer exercise due care and prudence in the preparation and implementation of its annual budget and observe the limitation on Personal Services in accordance with Section 325 of RA 7160. We also recommended that the Local Sanggunian see to it that appropriation ordinances comply with the aforementioned provision.
observations.managementAction:
Budget review of Annual Budget for fiscal year 2016 showed that there is an excess in the appropriation for Personal Services. 

The audit observation was first raised in Calendar Year (CY) 2015 AAR and reiterated in Part II – 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal officials immediately implement the projects during the year they were programmed.
observations.managementAction:
Various development projects were not completed/ implemented during the year.

The audit observation was first raised in CY 2012 AAR and reiterated in Part II – 3 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality create a team to conduct the inventory of property, plant and equipment, and prepare the required RPCPPE to be the basis for reconciliation with the accounting records.  Likewise, the conduct of physical inventory should be given attention in the incoming years in order to determine the actual status of the assets, whether still serviceable or should be disposed following the prescribed procedures.

By reason of non-implementation of the same prior year audit recommendation, we reiterated that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly observe the general limitations on Personal Services cited under Section 325 (a) of RA No. 7160.  Likewise, due care and diligence should be exercised in the formulation of Personal Services component of the annual budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mobilize the concerned offices of the LGU to fast track the implementation of various development projects contained in the Annual Investment Plan in accordance with the planned schedule of implementation and in conformity with the approved plans and specifications.  Likewise, the management should maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, program and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive being the MDRRMC Chairman direct the MDRRMO-Designate and other concerned officials to maximize the utilization of 70 percent Mitigation Fund by giving preference to the implementation of disaster related programs, projects and activities as planned in order to address any adverse effect of disaster.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC Chairperson to adopt competitive bidding as the general method of procurement in the procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants, and alternative methods shall be resorted only in highly exceptional cases with strict adherence to the prescribed requisites.

We recommended that cash advances for confidential/intelligence activities be limited only to the three (3) month requirement in accordance to Section 6.1.5 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCGOCC-DND Joint Circular dated January 8, 2015. We also recommended that disbursement vouchers and supporting documents of cash advances for confidential/intelligence activities be submitted to the COA Audit Team Leader within seven (7) working days from release of the check as required by Section 6.3.3.d of the circular.

We recommended that management exert effort in securing the records of the Other Receivables which remained outstanding for more than fifteen (15) years and enforce its collection.  In the event that its collections is improbable, procedures prescribed pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 should be followed.
observations.managementAction:
Resolution No. 196-2016 dated October 18, 2016 was passed approving the write-off of long outstanding Other Receivables.  

There is on-going preparation of documents to support the Request for Write-off.

The finding was first raised in CY 2009 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU – Patnongon, Antique fast-track the construction of the remaining core shelter units and upon completion, immediately award to family beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction:
For CY 2016, six (6) units were completed, seven (7) units are for roofing, and four (4) units are on-going. 

The finding was first raised in CY 2014 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU submit the required updated lacking supporting documents per Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 dated February 28, 2005.
observations.managementAction:
There is ongoing follow up of applications for special patent.

The finding was first raised in CY 2011 AAR.

observations.managementAction:
The request for write-off KASIPA account is on-process.

The finding was first raised in CY 2012 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Government Unit exert effort to have the purchased lots titled in the name of the municipality to establish ownership of the land.
observations.managementAction:

The finding was first raised in CY 2011 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive cause the refund of P920,000.00 for payment of Productivity Enhancement Incentive granted to officials, regular and casual employees inasmuch as disbursements exceeding the PS CAP constitute unauthorized benefits pursuant to Section 325(a) of Republic Act No. 7160.
